author: allexcd
description: Read-only, exclusion-based local bridge for using vault notes through MCP clients. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: allexcd
manifest.description: Read-only by default, exclusion-based local bridge for using vault notes through MCP clients.
manifest.name: MCP Vault Bridge

name: MCP Vault Bridge

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Added

- Added `create_base_file`, a new write tool for creating Obsidian Bases (`.base`) files.
- Bases can now be created for folders, tags, specific files, custom filters, or the whole vault.
- Added support for Bases columns, filters, exclusions, extensions, views, sorting, formulas, and summaries.
- Generated Bases exclude `.base` files by default so a base does not list itself.

### Improved

- Folder-based base requests now resolve the real vault folder before writing.
- Folder-scoped Bases are created inside the resolved folder by default, for example `Articles/Science/Science.base`.
- Missing folders are only created when the user clearly asks for a new folder.
- Tool guidance now better reflects Obsidian behavior for Properties, links, folders, and Bases.
- README was rewritten to make setup, tools, write access, Bases, privacy, and troubleshooting easier to understand.

### Fixed

- Prevented ambiguous folder prompts from creating misplaced root-level Bases.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

- Added `set_note_properties`, a new MCP write tool for updating Obsidian Properties/frontmatter through Obsidian’s own `processFrontMatter` API.
- Improved local LLM write flows so models are more likely to stop after a successful edit instead of looping through repeated read/search/write calls.
- Hardened `rewrite_note` recovery when models omit `path` after reading a note.
- Improved MCP client config generation so LM Studio/Claude configs prefer a Node.js command compatible with the installed SQLite runtime.

## New Functionality

### `set_note_properties`

- Sets Obsidian Properties/frontmatter on an existing note.
- Supports values like strings, numbers, booleans, `null`, and string arrays.
- Uses `null` for empty properties and `[]` for empty tags.
- Repairs malformed existing frontmatter when possible, then preserves the note body.

## Behavior Improvements

- Write tools now return clearer completion guidance, including when the edit is complete and when optional embedding maintenance can wait.
- `rewrite_note` can fall back to the last successfully read note path if the model leaves `path` blank.
- `replace_note_text` guidance now clearly says it is for body text, not Obsidian Properties.
- `rewrite_note` guidance now frames it as a whole-note replacement tool, not the default for partial edits.
- `set_note_properties` uses a simpler schema that works better with LM Studio/Gemma tool parsing.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

This patch release fixes MCP client config generation for LM Studio and Claude Desktop when Node.js is installed through a version manager or otherwise unavailable as plain `node` from the app environment.

The MCP Clients settings section now resolves the Node.js command dynamically and shows the absolute Node path in the visible config examples. Copy config uses the same resolved command, so pasted LM Studio configs no longer launch with `"command": "node"` when an absolute path is needed.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

This patch release improves Node.js runtime detection for users with multiple Node versions installed through version managers.

The plugin now prefers the active or profile-selected Node/npm path before falling back to discovered installed versions. This prevents older installed versions from being selected ahead of the currently active version.

## Runtime Detection

The resolver now prioritizes active paths for:

- nvm
- Volta
- asdf
- fnm
- mise
- Homebrew-linked Node installs

Installed-version scans are still used, but only as lower-priority fallbacks.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

This patch release fixes runtime setup on macOS when Obsidian cannot see `node` or `npm` from its app-launched environment.

The plugin now checks common managed Node.js install locations, including `nvm`, Homebrew, Volta, asdf, and fnm, before reporting Node/npm as unavailable. This should make the SQLite runtime install flow work for users whose Node.js install is available in Terminal but not in Obsidian.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

MCP Vault Bridge now includes opt-in write tools for MCP clients.

New tools:

- `create_note`
- `append_note`
- `replace_note_text`
- `delete_note_text`
- `rewrite_note`

Write tools remain disabled by default and must be explicitly enabled in the Obsidian plugin.

## Safety And Behavior

This release keeps the plugin read-only by default. When write tools are enabled, writes still respect:

- Vault scope protections
- Excluded folders, files, and tags
- Hidden paths
- `.obsidian`, `.trash`, and `.git` protections
- Path traversal protections

Exact-text edits avoid guessing. If matching text appears more than once, the tool requires an occurrence index before editing.

## Index And Embeddings

Write operations now update the local index after note changes.

This release also adds embedding maintenance support, including orphaned embedding pruning.

## Maintenance

- Refreshed runtime and transitive dependencies.
- Updated security-related dependency packages.
- Upgraded dev and test tooling, including Vitest 4 compatibility.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixes

- Addressed the remaining ObsidianReviewBot findings from the community plugin review.
- Cleaned up async test mocks and bridge helpers without changing runtime behavior.
- Adjusted Obsidian file/metadata typings used by the local bridge.
- Reworded a few settings messages to satisfy Obsidian UI text checks.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Highlights

- Added smarter vault-wide question handling with the new `ask_vault` MCP tool.
- Improved embedding usage so conceptual questions can use semantic search automatically when embeddings are enabled.
- Added clearer MCP client config examples, including an embeddings-enabled setup for LM Studio or other OpenAI-compatible embedding servers.
- Improved first-run behavior for Community Plugin installs: the plugin now installs as the standard three Obsidian files and materializes its local MCP runtime files after enablement.
- Added ObsidianReviewBot-focused cleanup for Community Plugin review readiness.
- Improved docs around indexing, embeddings, refresh behavior, and prompt examples.

### Notes

- Embeddings remain optional. Without them, the plugin still uses SQLite full-text search.
- With embeddings enabled, vectors are stored locally in the plugin’s SQLite index.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Fixes Obsidian Community Plugin installs by making the standard three installed files self-contained.
- Automatically creates the local `mcp-server.cjs` and runtime `package.json` after the plugin is enabled.
- Keeps `node_modules` out of the plugin bundle; SQLite dependencies are still installed only from plugin settings.
- Aligns the zip/build output with what Obsidian actually installs.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Updates the plugin metadata for Obsidian Community Plugins compatibility.
- Renames the public plugin identity to **MCP Vault Bridge**.
- Includes required release assets for community plugin installation.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## MCP Vault Bridge 0.3.0

### Highlights

- Renamed the plugin from **Obsidian MCP** to **MCP Vault Bridge** for community plugin submission.
- Changed the plugin id to `mcp-vault-bridge`, which follows Obsidian community plugin requirements.
- Updated build, install, package, and release assets to use the `mcp-vault-bridge` plugin folder and zip name.
- Kept the same read-only MCP bridge behavior introduced in `0.2.0`.

### Notes

- If you installed a previous manual build under `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-mcp`, install this release under `.obsidian/plugins/mcp-vault-bridge`.
- After switching folders, copy the token again from plugin settings and update LM Studio or Claude Desktop configs.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Highlights

- Added automatic first-run indexing so notes can appear without manually running `refresh_index`.
- Improved setup reliability with clearer bridge, runtime, and token status messages.
- Made bridge tokens safer by generating them per plugin install instead of reusing one global token.
- Fixed token copy/regenerate issues when Obsidian SecretStorage is unavailable or rejects a key.
- Improved bridge startup errors, including clearer messages for port conflicts.
- Fixed test/build reliability for the monorepo.

### Notes

- Embeddings are still off by default and must be enabled explicitly.
- Vault exclusions are still respected during automatic indexing.
- The plugin bundle still ships without `node_modules`; install runtime dependencies from settings.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What is this?

**Obsidian MCP** is a read-only bridge that connects your Obsidian vault to MCP-capable AI hosts — Claude Desktop and LM Studio. Once installed, your AI assistant can search, read, and navigate your notes without you copying and pasting anything.

## Features

- **Full-text search** across all included notes via a local SQLite index
- **Semantic search** with optional local embeddings (LM Studio + `nomic-embed-text-v1.5` or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint)
- **Read notes, metadata, links, and backlinks** by path
- **Exclusion-based privacy** — exclude folders, files, or tags before connecting any MCP host; hidden folders, `.obsidian`, `.trash`, and `.git` are always blocked
- **Token-gated bridge** — a bearer token generated by the plugin controls all access; stored in Obsidian SecretStorage when available
- **No write tools** — the plugin exposes no write, delete, shell, or command-execution capabilities
- **No telemetry**

## MCP tools

`vault_status` · `refresh_index` · `index_status` · `list_notes` · `search_vault` · `read_note` · `get_note_metadata` · `get_note_links` · `related_notes`

## Install

1. Download `obsidian-mcp-0.1.0.zip` below, unzip it, and copy the `obsidian-mcp` folder into your vault.

## Notes
- This is a developer preview. The plugin archive does not bundle `node_modules` — the `SQLite` runtime is downloaded on first use via the plugin settings.
- Keep Obsidian open while the MCP server is in use.
- Review your exclusions before connecting an MCP host. Vault content returned by tools may be forwarded to a cloud model by the host.
